name: NEXUS AI OS competitive position
summary: NEXUS is positioned as a three-product AI operating system: Code, PA OS, and Interview. The differentiator is product breadth plus privacy and sandbox-first architecture, while the main remaining gap is depth against mature single-purpose developer tools.

competitors.name: Cursor
competitors.nexus_response: NEXUS Code focuses on a web-first workspace with sandboxed execution, approval-first diffs, GitHub App flow, PA, and Interview in the same platform.

competitors.name: Claude Code / Claude Desktop
competitors.nexus_response: NEXUS aims to make agent work visible through jobs, diffs, preview checks, approvals, and product-specific contexts.

competitors.name: VS Code with Copilot
competitors.nexus_response: NEXUS must win with higher-level project execution, safer automation, and personal OS features rather than editor ecosystem alone.

competitors.name: ChatGPT Desktop / Voice
competitors.nexus_response: NEXUS PA and Interview focus on task ownership, resume-grounded coaching, privacy vault controls, and user-visible context.

evidence: Hub shows Code, PA, and Interview as separate products
capabilities.next: Add launch gating and onboarding metrics per product.
Deepen editor diagnostics, preview browser automation, symbol graph, and long-running job UX.
Run all preview/build/test workloads inside a production sandbox with persistent workspace artifacts.
Add per-hunk review UI, side-by-side diff viewer, and rollback browser.
Expand encrypted fields beyond vault-ready flows and add recovery/rotation UX.
Connect calendar/email providers, browser notifications, quiet hours, and durable automation worker.
Add scored interview drills, richer company intelligence, and latency instrumentation.
Wire real Stripe checkout/portal sessions, webhook signature verification, and org/team controls.

latency_targets.note: Sub-second full LLM answers are aspirational; NEXUS should optimize first visible signal, streaming, and cached context.

strategic_gaps: Production browser preview verification with screenshots and console/network evidence
Durable long-running background jobs across deploys
GitHub App install-to-PR flow hardened with real permissions and checks
Strict billing enforcement and admin controls
positioning: Do not claim NEXUS is a better Cursor today.
Position NEXUS as a web-first AI OS with Code, PA, and Interview.
Be transparent about implemented versus target capabilities.
Win by combining safe automation, personal context, and product-specific workflows.
